e: Pattern: Noun+に対して, Verb (plain form)+に対して, い-adjective+に対して, な-adjective+な/である/だった/であった+のに対して, Noun+に(対する/対しての)+Noun, 名+に対する+名. Up until now, Japan has taken a closed policy toward foreign countries.

s: The grammar pattern 〜て means '([verb]-te, connects two sentences)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜て' mean?
d: Try to do something, but the nuance is that it was unsuccessful.
I guess, wonder, probably, isn't it?
e: I'm going to the book shop to buy it.

s: The grammar pattern 条件形 means 'if'
q: What does the grammar pattern '条件形' mean?
e: If you go, you'll understand.

s: The grammar pattern なら means 'if'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'なら' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb (casual)+なら+(ば), い-adjective+なら+(ば), な-adjective (without な)+なら+(ば), Noun+なら+(ば), Assumed context+なら+Result. If it's from outside, I don't mind.

s: The grammar pattern させる means 'make, let someone do something'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'させる' mean?
d: toward; to; in contrast to; whereas; in regard to; in; per
to give me, to do for me, I was given
e: I made my daughter study

s: The grammar pattern 〜と means '(on when) doing, if'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜と' mean?
d: Try to do something, but the nuance is that it was unsuccessful.
of all the ... / especially / only / just
I had the intention to do (but I didn't)
e: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+と, Noun+だ+と. after you see ...

s: The grammar pattern 上げる means 'to do something for someone else'
q: What does the grammar pattern '上げる' mean?
a: to do something for someone else
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
you are required to complete this task
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
e: Pattern: Verb て-form+あげる. I will read this to you

s: The grammar pattern おかげで means 'thanks to'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'おかげで' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb casual form+おかけで, Noun+の+おかけで, い-adjective+おかけで, な-adjevtive+な+おかけで. It's thanks to you that I received the promotion.

s: The grammar pattern からして means '(1)Even; (2)Given, (Judging) From/By'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'からして' mean?
d: in the not too distant future
at the exact moment when something happens
e: (1) He can't even read hiragana.

s: The grammar pattern みたい means 'looks like'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'みたい' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb (casual)+みたい, Noun+みたい, い-adjective+みたい, な-adjective (without な)+みたい. Seems like he's caught a cold.

s: The grammar pattern べき means 'should do'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'べき' mean?
e: I should have studied more.

s: The grammar pattern 合う means 'Vm, verb~together, mutually'
q: What does the grammar pattern '合う' mean?
e: I talked it out with him.

s: The grammar pattern つい means 'without thinking, despite myself'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'つい' mean?
e: Pattern: つい+phrase (the thing done unintentionally). oops, i said too much!

s: The grammar pattern 為 means 'In order to, because of, for'
q: What does the grammar pattern '為' mean?
a: In order to, because of, for
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
you are required to complete this task
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
e: I telephoned to make sure he was coming.

s: The grammar pattern し means 'and'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'し' mean?
e: we went shopping, and ate lunch

s: The grammar pattern 〜中に、〜上に、〜下に、など means 'Talking about relative location of an object'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜中に、〜上に、〜下に、など' mean?
a: Talking about relative location of an object
d: Vte, (Definitely, completely [often to ones regret] do V)
center on, in the middle of
and then, after that; Due to that
because of, due to (indicates only negative meaning)
following; in accordance with; as 〜, accordingly; in proportion to
e: My hat is in your car

s: The grammar pattern まま means 'as it is, leave as it is, (remain) unchanged'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'まま' mean?
a: as it is, leave as it is, (remain) unchanged
d: I had the intention to do (but I didn't)
Try to do something, but the nuance is that it was unsuccessful.
absolutely, surely, by all means, no matter what, at any cost; after all
because of, due to (indicates only negative meaning)
side (of something, or taking someone's side)
e: Pattern: Verb た-form+まま, Verb ない-form+まま, い-adjective+まま, な-adjective+な+まま, Noun+の+まま. I'll leave this place as it is.

s: The grammar pattern あっと言う間 means 'In a flash, before one knows it, in a blink of an eye, in no time, flash by, (time) flies'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'あっと言う間' mean?
a: In a flash, before one knows it, in a blink of an eye, in no time, flash by, (time) flies
d: when you talk of, speaking of, when you say
while doing A, I had a chance to do B, since doing A anyway, do B
To be, is (formal equivalent of だ [da])
e: Time flies when you're having fun

s: The grammar pattern とする means 'assume, regard X as ~ , suppose'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'とする' mean?
d: time and place prepositions at in on
Adds emphasis - sentence ending particle; Emphasizes personal desires
as well as; with; along with; at the same time; when; as; while
e: if we assume that n=1

s: The grammar pattern 〜か means 'whether, the question of (sub sentence question)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜か' mean?
a: whether, the question of (sub sentence question)
d: Talking about relative location of an object
must ____; really should _____; it won't do if you don't ____.
I had the intention to do (but I didn't)
I wonder if...　（A type of question mark)
e: Regardless of whether I had written it...
